page: Microsoft Azure E96bds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EBDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 48 MB of L3 cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1D cache per core. The system is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 3,686 GB of local disk capacity and is Premium Storage capable, designed for high block storage performance.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves strong results in multi-core CPU stress tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Passmark CPU benchmarks, including a top-tier rating in CPU encryption. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only cached small block speeds reaching 5005.81 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 139.89 GB/sec. However, single-core performance remains average across stress-ng and Geekbench tests. While the instance handles small to medium LLM inference prompt processing with strong to average speeds, text generation for large 70B models is a notable tradeoff, registering a weak performance of 2.73 tokens/sec due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E96bds_v5 offers a high-density resource profile, combining substantial memory capacity with a large local storage allocation. This configuration is highly suitable for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads without requiring the higher cost of GPU-accelerated instances. Appropriate workloads include large-scale database operations, high-throughput Redis deployments, static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E96bds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EBDSv5 family, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores) and 672.0 GB of RAM. It features 3,686 GB of local storage with premium block storage capabilities. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU execution, high memory bandwidth, and top-tier encryption performance, alongside strong database and Redis operations. However, single-core processing and large-scale LLM text generation are moderate to weak due to the lack of GPU acceleration. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for high-density memory and storage workloads, making it highly suitable for large databases, multi-threaded software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96bds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 672 GB of RAM, and 3,686 GB of local storage, designed for high-performance database and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96bds_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 3,686 GB local storage for database and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory and local storage virtual machine optimized for demanding database operations and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 672 GB of system memory
3,686 GB of local storage with premium block storage capabilities
Strong multi-core performance and high-bandwidth memory read/write speeds
Top-tier cryptographic execution and strong Redis database throughput
Suited for large databases, software compilation, and static web serving
Weak performance on large-scale LLM text generation due to lack of GPU
